cases.input: A department manager wants to build an app to track employee training completions. They have Power Apps licenses and no development team. Which stack should they use?
cases.context: Power Apps licensed, M365 tenant, 50 users, no IT developers, data in SharePoint lists currently

cases.input: We need to build a customer-facing REST API that integrates with our Power Platform workflows and requires custom business logic that Power Automate cannot handle alone. What stack do we select?
cases.context: 200,000 external API consumers, complex pricing logic, existing Power Platform deployment, Azure subscription available, 24/7 SLA required

cases.input: We're building a real-time financial trading platform with microservices, event streaming, and strict latency requirements. Select the appropriate Microsoft stack.
cases.context: Sub-100ms latency required, 500,000 transactions per day, Kubernetes expertise in team, Azure subscription, Kafka-style messaging needed

cases.input: A manufacturing company needs to replace their legacy ERP for finance and supply chain management while also modernizing their sales CRM. Help them select the right D365 stack composition.
cases.context: 2000 employees, 3 regions, existing legacy ERP, D365 licenses budgeted, Power BI for reporting, Azure available

cases.input: A startup says they want Stack A (Power Apps only) for their new B2C e-commerce platform expecting 50,000 daily users with real-time inventory, payment processing, and personalized recommendations.
cases.context: Stack A initially requested, B2C scale, payment processing required, real-time inventory, AI recommendations planned
